_meta.source: Hand-curated from ICAR Kharif/Rabi calendars and state agriculture department advisories. Karnataka windows reflect southern peninsula timing; Haryana windows reflect Indo-Gangetic belt. Where a crop has multiple seasons, the dominant local season is given.

Ragi.critical_weather: Needs 600–1000 mm rainfall during tillering (Jul–Aug). Drought-tolerant but pod-fill stage suffers if monsoon withdraws early.

Paddy.critical_weather: Needs standing water 5–10 cm during vegetative stage. Heavy rain at flowering can cause grain shattering.

Tur.critical_weather: Drought-tolerant; critical pod-fill in Oct–Nov needs no waterlogging. Pod-borer risk during flowering.

Jowar.critical_weather: Drought-tolerant kharif crop; needs ~500 mm rainfall. Excess rain at grain-fill causes grain mould.

Maize.critical_weather: Needs ~600 mm well-distributed rainfall; tasselling/silking (Aug–Sep) is the most water-sensitive phase.

Sugarcane.critical_weather: 12–14 month crop; needs irrigation during tillering (Apr–Jun). Frost is rare in KA but yield drops if Dec–Jan night temps fall below 10 °C.

Cotton.critical_weather: Bollworm risk pre-monsoon; July–Aug rains help boll development. Excess rain at boll-opening (Oct–Dec) reduces lint quality.

Groundnut.critical_weather: Pegging stage (Aug) needs moist soil. Dry, sunny weather essential at harvest — rain on lifted plants causes aflatoxin risk.

Bengal Gram (Chana).critical_weather: Cool dry weather essential during pod-fill (Jan–Feb). Late winter rain promotes pod-borer.

Green Gram (Moong).critical_weather: Sensitive to waterlogging. 60–90 day crop — heavy rain at pod-fill (Aug) reduces yield sharply.

Onion.critical_weather: Bulb-formation (Feb–Mar) needs cool dry days. Late rains at harvest cause storage rot.

Tomato.critical_weather: Sensitive to frost and heat above 35 °C. Heavy rain causes flower drop and fruit cracking.

Potato.critical_weather: Needs cool weather (15–25 °C) for tuber bulking. Frost-sensitive in north KA highlands.

Wheat.critical_weather: Cool weather (Dec–Feb) essential for tillering. Heat stress in Mar (>35 °C at grain-fill) sharply reduces yield.

Paddy.critical_weather: Heavy water requirement. Monsoon withdrawal in Sep should coincide with grain-fill — early withdrawal reduces yield.

Mustard.critical_weather: Cool dry weather Nov–Jan ideal. Aphid attack risk in Jan–Feb if humidity high.

Bajra.critical_weather: Tolerates 350–400 mm rainfall. Excess rain at panicle-emergence (Aug) causes ergot disease.

Cotton.critical_weather: Pre-monsoon irrigation crucial. Monsoon rains (Jul–Aug) drive boll development. Frost in Dec damages late-picking.

Sugarcane.critical_weather: Cool nights post-Nov increase sugar recovery. Frost in Dec–Jan damages cane yield in HY belt.

Bengal Gram (Chana).critical_weather: Cool dry weather Dec–Feb essential. Late-winter rain promotes pod-borer; frost can damage flowering.

Groundnut.critical_weather: Pegging needs moist soil (Aug). Dry, sunny harvest weather essential to avoid aflatoxin.

Onion.critical_weather: Cool dry weather Dec–Feb for bulb formation. Pre-harvest rain causes storage losses.

Tomato.critical_weather: Frost-sensitive in Dec–Jan. Heat above 35 °C causes flower drop in zaid season.

Potato.critical_weather: Cool weather (15–25 °C) ideal for tuber bulking. Frost in Dec–Jan damages foliage; late rain causes late-blight.
